title: aster coerulescens var. subasper
intro: Aster coerulescens var. subasper, also known as the Wood's Aster, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of North America, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and woody stems. The medicinal properties of Aster coerulescens var. subasper include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, bruises, and sore muscles. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
